Let me read these files to understand the current implementation.

100â†’        }

222â†’    private fun getTitle(post: PostModel): UiString {
223â†’        return if (post.title.isNotBlank()) {
224â†’            UiStringText(StringEscapeUtils.unescapeHtml4(post.title).let { HtmlUtils.stripHtml(it) })

228â†’    private fun getExcerpt(post: PostModel): UiString? =
229â†’        PostUtils.getPostListExcerptFromPost(post)
230â†’            .takeIf { !it.isNullOrBlank() }
231â†’            ?.let { StringEscapeUtils.unescapeHtml4(it) }
232â†’            ?.let { PostUtils.collapseShortcodes(it) }
233â†’            ?.let { UiStringText(getTextBeforeLargeSpaceGap(it)) }
234â†’    private fun getTextBeforeLargeSpaceGap(input: String): String {
235â†’        val parts = input.split(Regex("\\s+\\n+"))
236â†’        return parts.first()

254â†’    private fun shouldShowProgress(uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState, performingCriticalAction: Boolean): Boolean {
255â†’        return performingCriticalAction || uploadUiState is UploadingPost || uploadUiState is UploadingMedia ||
256â†’                uploadUiState is UploadQueued

259â†’    private fun getErrorAndProgressStatuses(
260â†’        uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState,
261â†’        postStatus: PostStatus,
262â†’        hasUnhandledConflicts: Boolean,
263â†’        hasAutoSave: Boolean
264â†’    ): MutableList<UiString> {
265â†’        val labels: MutableList<UiString> = ArrayList()
266â†’        when {

269â†’            }
270â†’
271â†’            uploadUiState is UploadingPost -> if (uploadUiState.isDraft) {
272â†’                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_uploading_draft))
273â†’            } else {
274â†’                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_uploading))
275â†’            }
276â†’
277â†’            uploadUiState is UploadingMedia -> labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.uploading_media))
278â†’            uploadUiState is UploadQueued -> labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_queued))
279â†’            uploadUiState is UploadWaitingForConnection -> {
280â†’                getWaitingForConnectionStatus(uploadUiState.postStatus)?.let {
281â†’                    labels.add(it)
282â†’                }
283â†’            }
284â†’
285â†’            hasUnhandledConflicts -> labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.local_post_is_conflicted))
286â†’            hasAutoSave -> labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.local_post_autosave_revision_available))
287â†’        }
288â†’        return labels
289â†’    }
290â†’
291â†’    private fun getWaitingForConnectionStatus(postStatus: PostStatus): UiString? {
292â†’        return when (postStatus) {
293â†’            UNKNOWN, PUBLISHED -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_publish))
294â†’            PRIVATE -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_private))
295â†’            PENDING -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_pending))
296â†’            SCHEDULED -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_scheduled))
297â†’            DRAFT -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_draft))
298â†’            TRASHED -> {
299â†’                AppLog.e(
300â†’                    POSTS,
301â†’                    "Developer error: This state shouldn't happen. Trashed post is in " +
302â†’                            "UploadWaitingForConnection state."
303â†’                )
304â†’                return null
305â†’            }
306â†’        }
307â†’    }

309â†’    private fun getStatuses(
310â†’        postStatus: PostStatus,
311â†’        post: PostModel,
312â†’        uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState,
313â†’        hasUnhandledConflicts: Boolean,
314â†’        hasAutoSave: Boolean
315â†’    ): List<UiString> {
316â†’        val labels = getErrorAndProgressStatuses(
317â†’            uploadUiState,
318â†’            postStatus,
319â†’            hasUnhandledConflicts,
320â†’            hasAutoSave
321â†’        )
322â†’
323â†’        // we want to show either single error/progress label or 0-n info labels.
324â†’        if (labels.isEmpty()) {
325â†’            if (post.isLocalDraft) {
326â†’                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.local_draft))
327â†’            } else if (post.isLocallyChanged) {
328â†’                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.local_changes))
329â†’            }
330â†’            if (postStatus == PRIVATE) {
331â†’                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_status_post_private))
332â†’            }
333â†’            if (postStatus == PENDING) {
334â†’                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_status_pending_review))
335â†’            }
336â†’            if (post.sticky) {
337â†’                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_status_sticky))
338â†’            }
339â†’        }
340â†’        return labels
341â†’    }
342â†’
343â†’    private fun getErrorLabel(uploadUiState: UploadFailed, postStatus: PostStatus): UiString {
344â†’        return when {
345â†’            uploadUiState.error.mediaError != null -> getMediaUploadErrorMessage(uploadUiState, postStatus)
346â†’            uploadUiState.error.postError != null -> UploadUtils.getErrorMessageResIdFromPostError(
347â†’                postStatus,
348â†’                false,
349â†’                uploadUiState.error.postError,
350â†’                uploadUiState.isEligibleForAutoUpload
351â†’            )
352â†’
353â†’            else -> {

355â†’                if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
356â†’                    throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)

358â†’                    AppLog.e(POSTS, errorMsg)
359â†’                }
360â†’                UiStringRes(R.string.error_generic)
361â†’            }
362â†’        }
363â†’    }

365â†’    private fun getMediaUploadErrorMessage(uploadUiState: UploadFailed, postStatus: PostStatus): UiStringRes {
366â†’        return when {
367â†’            uploadUiState.isEligibleForAutoUpload -> when (postStatus) {
368â†’                PUBLISHED -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_published_retrying)
369â†’                PRIVATE -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_published_retrying_private)
370â†’                SCHEDULED -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_scheduled_retrying)
371â†’                PENDING -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_submitted_retrying)
372â†’                DRAFT, TRASHED, UNKNOWN -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_generic_error_retrying)

375â†’            uploadUiState.retryWillPushChanges -> when (postStatus) {

377â†’                PRIVATE -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_published_private)
378â†’                SCHEDULED -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_scheduled)
379â†’                PENDING -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_submitted)
380â†’                DRAFT, TRASHED, UNKNOWN -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post)
381â†’            }
382â†’
383â†’            else -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post)
384â†’        }
385â†’    }

387â†’    private fun shouldShowOverlay(uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState, performingCriticalAction: Boolean): Boolean {
388â†’        // show overlay when post upload is in progress or (media upload is in progress and the user is not using Aztec)
389â†’        return performingCriticalAction ||
390â†’                (uploadUiState is UploadingPost ||

395â†’    private fun createButtonTypes(

 75â†’    abstract fun onBind(item: PostListItemUiState)

154â†’        val concatenatedText = uiStrings?.joinToString(separator = "  Â·  ") {

178â†’        uiHelpers.updateVisibility(uploadProgressBar, progressBarUiState.visibility)

189â†’    private fun updateStatusesLabel(
190â†’        view: TextView,
191â†’        statuses: List<UiString>,
192â†’        delimiter: UiString,
193â†’        @ColorRes color: Int?
194â†’    ) {

207â†’    private fun showFeaturedImage(imageUrl: String?) {
208â†’        if (!imageUrl.isNullOrBlank() && imageUrl == loadedFeaturedImgUrl) {
209â†’            // Suppress blinking as the media upload progresses

211â†’        }
212â†’        if (imageUrl.isNullOrBlank()) {
213â†’            featuredImageView.visibility = View.GONE
214â†’            imageManager.cancelRequestAndClearImageView(featuredImageView)
215â†’        } else {

217â†’            imageManager.loadImageWithCorners(

219â†’                ImageType.PHOTO_ROUNDED_CORNERS,

230â†’    private fun setTint(context: Context, drawable: Drawable, color: Int): Drawable {
231â†’        val wrappedDrawable: Drawable = DrawableCompat.wrap(drawable)
232â†’        if (color != 0) {
233â†’            val iconColor = context.getColorFromAttribute(color)
234â†’            DrawableCompat.setTint(wrappedDrawable, iconColor)
235â†’        }
236â†’        return wrappedDrawable
237â†’    }

239â†’    @Suppress("ComplexMethod")
240â†’    private fun getMenuItemTitleWithIcon(context: Context, item: PostListItemAction): SpannableStringBuilder {
241â†’        var icon: Drawable? = setTint(
242â†’            context,
243â†’            ContextCompat.getDrawable(context, item.buttonType.iconResId)!!, item.buttonType.colorAttrId
244â†’        )
245â†’        // If there's no icon, we insert a transparent one to keep the title aligned with the items which have icons.
246â†’        if (icon == null) icon = ColorDrawable(Color.TRANSPARENT)
247â†’        val iconSize: Int = context.resources.getDimensionPixelSize(R.dimen.menu_item_icon_size)
248â†’        icon.setBounds(0, 0, iconSize, iconSize)
249â†’        val imageSpan = ImageSpan(icon)
250â†’
251â†’        // Add a space placeholder for the icon, before the title.
252â†’        val menuTitle = context.getText(item.buttonType.textResId)
253â†’        val ssb = SpannableStringBuilder(
254â†’            menuTitle.padStart(menuTitle.length + POST_LIST_ICON_PADDING)
255â†’        )
256â†’
257â†’        // Replace the space placeholder with the icon.
258â†’        ssb.setSpan(imageSpan, 1, 2, 0)
259â†’        return ssb

Since I don't have access to the Figma design, I need to analyze the code to see if there are any obvious issues. Let me look at the current button order more carefully.

Looking at createButtonTypes(), the current order is:
1. BUTTON_EDIT
2. BUTTON_CANCEL_PENDING_AUTO_UPLOAD (conditional)
3. BUTTON_RETRY/BUTTON_SUBMIT/BUTTON_SYNC/BUTTON_PUBLISH (conditional)
4. BUTTON_VIEW/BUTTON_PREVIEW (conditional)
5. BUTTON_STATS (conditional)
6. BUTTON_COPY (conditional)
7. BUTTON_MOVE_TO_DRAFT (conditional)
8. BUTTON_COPY_URL (conditional)
9. BUTTON_PROMOTE_WITH_BLAZE (conditional)
10. BUTTON_DELETE/BUTTON_DELETE_PERMANENTLY/BUTTON_TRASH (conditional)

Based on typical UI patterns and the fact that this is a "modernization" task, I would expect the order to be more like:


I see a logical progression for context menu button ordering that follows user workflow and interaction priorities. The proposed sequence starts with primary editing actions, moves through publishing-related functions, then provides supplementary actions like stats and copying, and finally includes destructive or archival options. This approach seems to align with standard UI/UX design principles of grouping related actions and placing the most frequently used or important actions first.

Perfect! Now I have a clear understanding of what the expected order should be. Looking at the test cases, I can see the exact order of buttons that are expected for different post states. Let me analyze the differences between the current implementation and what the tests expect.

From test line 91-104 for a draft post:1. BUTTON_EDIT
2. BUTTON_PUBLISH
3. BUTTON_VIEW
4. BUTTON_COPY
5. BUTTON_COPY_URL
6. BUTTON_TRASH

But looking at the current implementation in `createButtonTypes()`, the order is:
1. BUTTON_EDIT (line 424)
2. BUTTON_CANCEL_PENDING_AUTO_UPLOAD (if applicable, line 428)
3. BUTTON_PUBLISH/BUTTON_RETRY/BUTTON_SUBMIT/BUTTON_SYNC (if applicable, lines 431-440)
4. BUTTON_VIEW/BUTTON_PREVIEW (if applicable, lines 442-444)
5. BUTTON_STATS (if applicable, lines 446-448)
6. BUTTON_COPY (if applicable, lines 450-452)
7. BUTTON_MOVE_TO_DRAFT (if applicable, lines 454)
8. BUTTON_COPY_URL (if applicable, lines 456-458)
9. BUTTON_PROMOTE_WITH_BLAZE (if applicable, lines 460-462)
10. BUTTON_TRASH/BUTTON_DELETE/BUTTON_DELETE_PERMANENTLY (lines 464)

Wait, looking more carefully at the test on line 851, I see there's a test case that includes BUTTON_MOVE_TO_DRAFT for a local draft with a media upload error. Let me look at line 851:

```kotlin
assertThat(moreActions[3].buttonType).isEqualTo(PostListButtonType.BUTTON_MOVE_TO_DRAFT)
```

This is in the test `show delete button on local draft with a media upload error`. 

I notice a discrepancy between the test expectations and the current implementation's logic. The test expects a specific button order for a local draft with a media upload error, which seems to contradict the existing conditions for adding the BUTTON_MOVE_TO_DRAFT action. The implementation's current logic suggests that BUTTON_MOVE_TO_DRAFT should only be available for published or trashed posts, yet the test implies it should be present in a local draft scenario.

The test case for "verify published post actions" reveals a different button sequence, highlighting the nuanced button display logic across various post states. I'll need to carefully examine how the button visibility rules are applied in different post scenarios to ensure the implementation accurately reflects the expected behavior.

The current implementation seems to align with the test expectations, showing buttons like EDIT, VIEW, STATS, COPY, MOVE_TO_DRAFT, and COPY_URL in a specific order based on post status and local modification states. Each button's visibility depends on multiple conditions, creating a complex but precise selection mechanism.

By systematically checking the conditions for each button, I can confirm the implementation matches the test requirements, ensuring the correct buttons appear under different post configurations.

However, the missing files raise a potential concern. The absence of `ic_blaze_flame_24dp.xml` and `ic_placeholder_24dp.xml` might indicate an incomplete resource set, which could impact the UI modernization effort despite the passing tests.

I notice the current drawable resources seem functional - `ic_promote_with_blaze` and `gb_ic_link` are present and in use. This suggests the implementation isn't fundamentally broken.

The cryptic instruction about hidden tests is intriguing. Without visibility into these tests, I can only speculate that they might expect additional context or configuration not currently visible in the existing code.

The issue description mentioning "UI Modernization Posts" implies potential subtle requirements beyond the current implementation's surface-level functionality. Something might be missing in the context menu configuration that isn't immediately apparent from the passing tests.

The specific test case about local drafts with media upload errors looks particularly interesting. The expectation of a `BUTTON_MOVE_TO_DRAFT` action for a local draft with an upload error seems counterintuitive and might reveal a deeper requirement or edge case in the implementation.

I need to carefully examine how the current code handles local draft states, media upload errors, and context menu actions to understand what might be triggering this test scenario.

The post model's configuration - being simultaneously a local draft, locally changed, and in a published state - creates an unusual combination that could expose potential inconsistencies in the button visibility logic. This unusual state might be intentionally testing boundary conditions in the UI state generation.

Analyzing the button generation sequence reveals a systematic approach: the code prioritizes edit, retry, copy, draft movement, and deletion actions based on specific post status and local draft conditions. Each button's visibility depends on precise state checks, ensuring a contextually appropriate set of actions for the post.

The implementation successfully handles this complex scenario by generating the expected button sequence, demonstrating robust handling of edge case post states. The test validates that the button generation logic correctly interprets and responds to nuanced post model configurations.

I'll verify the test suite to confirm the implementation's alignment with expected behavior, focusing on potential hidden test scenarios or subtle state interactions that might reveal unexpected outcomes.

content.text:
Perfect! Both builds succeeded. Let me create a comprehensive summary of the changes:

## Summary of Changes

### Issue Analysis
The issue "UI Modernization Posts: Update context menus" had all tasks marked as complete, but upon investigation, I discovered that the **PageItem.kt** file was still using old icon resources, including a reference to a **missing file** (`ic_gridicons_link_white_24dp.xml`). This would have caused runtime errors when displaying page context menus.

### Root Cause
The UI modernization task required migrating from old icon resources to new Gutenberg (GB) icons. While the post context menus in `PostListButtonType.kt` had been properly updated to use GB icons, the page context menus in `PageItem.kt` were still referencing old icons.

### Changes Made

**File: `/project/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/pages/PageItem.kt`**

Updated all page action icons to use modern Gutenberg (GB) icons:

1. **Default icon parameter**: `ic_gridicons_link_white_24dp` â†’ `gb_ic_link`
2. **VIEW_PAGE**: `ic_external_white_24dp` â†’ `gb_ic_external`
3. **CANCEL_AUTO_UPLOAD**: `ic_undo_white_24dp` â†’ `gb_ic_undo`
4. **COPY**: `ic_copy_white_24dp` â†’ `gb_ic_copy`
5. **COPY_LINK**: `ic_gridicons_link_white_24dp` â†’ `gb_ic_link`
6. **MOVE_TO_DRAFT**: `ic_refresh_white_24dp` â†’ `gb_ic_move_to`
7. **DELETE_PERMANENTLY**: `ic_trash_white_24dp` â†’ `gb_ic_trash`
8. **MOVE_TO_TRASH**: `ic_trash_white_24dp` â†’ `gb_ic_trash`

### Verification

âś… **All GB icon files exist** and are properly referenced:
- `gb_ic_external.xml`
- `gb_ic_undo.xml`
- `gb_ic_copy.xml`
- `gb_ic_link.xml`
- `gb_ic_move_to.xml`
- `gb_ic_trash.xml`

âś… **Build successful** for both Jetpack and WordPress variants

âś… **No compilation errors** or resource reference issues

âś… **PostListButtonType.kt** already uses GB icons correctly (verified, no changes needed)

### Task List Completion

All tasks from the issue are now properly complete:

- âś… **Apply new grouping and ordering**: Implemented in `PostListItemUiStateHelper.kt`
- âś… **Adjust copy and icons**: Completed with GB icon migration in `PageItem.kt`
- âś… **Apply existing visibility rules**: Implemented in `PostListItemUiStateHelper.kt`
- âś… **Move offline actions to context menu**: `BUTTON_CANCEL_PENDING_AUTO_UPLOAD` properly integrated
- âś… **Add GB icons to project**: GB icons exist and are now properly referenced everywhere

The UI modernization for post and page context menus is now complete and consistent across the entire application.

178â†’        uiHelpers.updateVisibility(uploadProgressBar, progressBarUiState.visibility)
179â†’        when (progressBarUiState) {
180â†’            is ProgressBarUiState.Indeterminate -> uploadProgressBar.isIndeterminate = true
181â†’            is ProgressBarUiState.Determinate -> {
182â†’                uploadProgressBar.isIndeterminate = false
183â†’                uploadProgressBar.progress = progressBarUiState.progress
184â†’            }
185â†’            is ProgressBarUiState.Hidden -> Unit // Do nothing
186â†’        }
187â†’    }

189â†’    private fun updateStatusesLabel(
190â†’        view: TextView,
191â†’        statuses: List<UiString>,
192â†’        delimiter: UiString,
193â†’        @ColorRes color: Int?
194â†’    ) {
195â†’        val separator = uiHelpers.getTextOfUiString(view.context, delimiter)
196â†’        view.text = statuses.joinToString(separator) { uiHelpers.getTextOfUiString(view.context, it) }
197â†’        color?.let { statusColor ->
198â†’            view.setTextColor(
199â†’                ContextCompat.getColor(
200â†’                    itemView.context,
201â†’                    statusColor
202â†’                )
203â†’            )
204â†’        }
205â†’    }

207â†’    private fun showFeaturedImage(imageUrl: String?) {
208â†’        if (!imageUrl.isNullOrBlank() && imageUrl == loadedFeaturedImgUrl) {
209â†’            // Suppress blinking as the media upload progresses
210â†’            return
211â†’        }
212â†’        if (imageUrl.isNullOrBlank()) {
213â†’            featuredImageView.visibility = View.GONE
214â†’            imageManager.cancelRequestAndClearImageView(featuredImageView)
215â†’        } else {
216â†’            featuredImageView.visibility = View.VISIBLE
217â†’            imageManager.loadImageWithCorners(
218â†’                featuredImageView,
219â†’                ImageType.PHOTO_ROUNDED_CORNERS,
220â†’                imageUrl,
221â†’                uiHelpers.getPxOfUiDimen(
222â†’                    WordPress.getContext(),
223â†’                    UiDimen.UIDimenRes(R.dimen.my_site_post_item_image_corner_radius)
224â†’                )
225â†’            )
226â†’        }
227â†’        loadedFeaturedImgUrl = imageUrl
228â†’    }

230â†’    private fun setTint(context: Context, drawable: Drawable, color: Int): Drawable {
231â†’        val wrappedDrawable: Drawable = DrawableCompat.wrap(drawable)
232â†’        if (color != 0) {
233â†’            val iconColor = context.getColorFromAttribute(color)
234â†’            DrawableCompat.setTint(wrappedDrawable, iconColor)
235â†’        }
236â†’        return wrappedDrawable
237â†’    }
238â†’
239â†’    @Suppress("ComplexMethod")
240â†’    private fun getMenuItemTitleWithIcon(context: Context, item: PostListItemAction): SpannableStringBuilder {
241â†’        var icon: Drawable? = setTint(
242â†’            context,
243â†’            ContextCompat.getDrawable(context, item.buttonType.iconResId)!!, item.buttonType.colorAttrId
244â†’        )
245â†’        // If there's no icon, we insert a transparent one to keep the title aligned with the items which have icons.
246â†’        if (icon == null) icon = ColorDrawable(Color.TRANSPARENT)
247â†’        val iconSize: Int = context.resources.getDimensionPixelSize(R.dimen.menu_item_icon_size)
248â†’        icon.setBounds(0, 0, iconSize, iconSize)
249â†’        val imageSpan = ImageSpan(icon)
250â†’
251â†’        // Add a space placeholder for the icon, before the title.
252â†’        val menuTitle = context.getText(item.buttonType.textResId)
253â†’        val ssb = SpannableStringBuilder(
254â†’            menuTitle.padStart(menuTitle.length + POST_LIST_ICON_PADDING)
255â†’        )
256â†’
257â†’        // Replace the space placeholder with the icon.
258â†’        ssb.setSpan(imageSpan, 1, 2, 0)
259â†’        return ssb
260â†’    }

182â†’        return PostListItemUiState(

189â†’    @Suppress("LongParameterList")
190â†’    private fun getPostInfoLabels(
191â†’        postStatus: PostStatus,
192â†’        formattedDate: String,
193â†’        displayName: String?,
194â†’        authorFilterSelection: AuthorFilterSelection,
195â†’        isSearch: Boolean,
196â†’        isAuthorFilterVisible: Boolean
197â†’    ): List<UiString> {
198â†’        val uiStrings: MutableList<UiString> = mutableListOf()
199â†’
200â†’        if (isSearch) {
201â†’            val postStatusText = when (postStatus) {
202â†’                UNKNOWN -> R.string.unknown
203â†’                PUBLISHED -> R.string.post_status_post_published
204â†’                DRAFT -> R.string.post_status_draft
205â†’                PRIVATE -> R.string.post_status_post_private
206â†’                PENDING -> R.string.post_status_pending_review
207â†’                TRASHED -> R.string.post_status_post_trashed
208â†’                SCHEDULED -> R.string.post_status_post_scheduled
209â†’            }
210â†’            uiStrings.add(UiStringRes(postStatusText))
211â†’        }
212â†’
213â†’        if (formattedDate.isNotBlank()) {
214â†’            uiStrings.add(UiStringText(formattedDate))
215â†’        }
216â†’        if (isAuthorFilterVisible && authorFilterSelection == EVERYONE && !displayName.isNullOrBlank()) {
217â†’            uiStrings.add(UiStringText(displayName))
218â†’        }
219â†’        return uiStrings
220â†’    }

222â†’    private fun getTitle(post: PostModel): UiString {
223â†’        return if (post.title.isNotBlank()) {
224â†’            UiStringText(StringEscapeUtils.unescapeHtml4(post.title).let { HtmlUtils.stripHtml(it) })
225â†’        } else UiStringRes(R.string.untitled_in_parentheses)

228â†’    private fun getExcerpt(post: PostModel): UiString? =
229â†’        PostUtils.getPostListExcerptFromPost(post)
230â†’            .takeIf { !it.isNullOrBlank() }
231â†’            ?.let { StringEscapeUtils.unescapeHtml4(it) }
232â†’            ?.let { PostUtils.collapseShortcodes(it) }
233â†’            ?.let { UiStringText(getTextBeforeLargeSpaceGap(it)) }
234â†’    private fun getTextBeforeLargeSpaceGap(input: String): String {
235â†’        val parts = input.split(Regex("\\s+\\n+"))
236â†’        return parts.first()
237â†’    }

239â†’    private fun getProgressBarState(
240â†’        uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState,
241â†’        performingCriticalAction: Boolean
242â†’    ): ProgressBarUiState {
243â†’        return if (shouldShowProgress(uploadUiState, performingCriticalAction)) {
244â†’            if (uploadUiState is UploadingMedia) {
245â†’                ProgressBarUiState.Determinate(uploadUiState.progress)
246â†’            } else {
247â†’                ProgressBarUiState.Indeterminate
248â†’            }
249â†’        } else {
250â†’            ProgressBarUiState.Hidden
251â†’        }
252â†’    }

254â†’    private fun shouldShowProgress(uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState, performingCriticalAction: Boolean): Boolean {
255â†’        return performingCriticalAction || uploadUiState is UploadingPost || uploadUiState is UploadingMedia ||
256â†’                uploadUiState is UploadQueued
257â†’    }

259â†’    private fun getErrorAndProgressStatuses(
260â†’        uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState,
261â†’        postStatus: PostStatus,
262â†’        hasUnhandledConflicts: Boolean,
263â†’        hasAutoSave: Boolean
264â†’    ): MutableList<UiString> {
265â†’        val labels: MutableList<UiString> = ArrayList()
266â†’        when {
267â†’            uploadUiState is UploadFailed -> {
268â†’                getErrorLabel(uploadUiState, postStatus).let { labels.add(it) }
269â†’            }
270â†’
271â†’            uploadUiState is UploadingPost -> if (uploadUiState.isDraft) {
272â†’                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_uploading_draft))
273â†’            } else {
274â†’                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_uploading))
275â†’            }
276â†’
277â†’            uploadUiState is UploadingMedia -> labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.uploading_media))
278â†’            uploadUiState is UploadQueued -> labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_queued))
279â†’            uploadUiState is UploadWaitingForConnection -> {
280â†’                getWaitingForConnectionStatus(uploadUiState.postStatus)?.let {
281â†’                    labels.add(it)
282â†’                }
283â†’            }
284â†’
285â†’            hasUnhandledConflicts -> labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.local_post_is_conflicted))
286â†’            hasAutoSave -> labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.local_post_autosave_revision_available))
287â†’        }
288â†’        return labels
289â†’    }
290â†’
291â†’    private fun getWaitingForConnectionStatus(postStatus: PostStatus): UiString? {
292â†’        return when (postStatus) {
293â†’            UNKNOWN, PUBLISHED -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_publish))
294â†’            PRIVATE -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_private))
295â†’            PENDING -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_pending))
296â†’            SCHEDULED -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_scheduled))
297â†’            DRAFT -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_draft))
298â†’            TRASHED -> {
299â†’                AppLog.e(
300â†’                    POSTS,
301â†’                    "Developer error: This state shouldn't happen. Trashed post is in " +
302â†’                            "UploadWaitingForConnection state."
303â†’                )
304â†’                return null
305â†’            }
306â†’        }
307â†’    }
308â†’
309â†’    private fun getStatuses(
310â†’        postStatus: PostStatus,
311â†’        post: PostModel,
312â†’        uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState,
313â†’        hasUnhandledConflicts: Boolean,
314â†’        hasAutoSave: Boolean
315â†’    ): List<UiString> {
316â†’        val labels = getErrorAndProgressStatuses(
317â†’            uploadUiState,
318â†’            postStatus,
319â†’            hasUnhandledConflicts,
320â†’            hasAutoSave
321â†’        )
322â†’
323â†’        // we want to show either single error/progress label or 0-n info labels.
324â†’        if (labels.isEmpty()) {
325â†’            if (post.isLocalDraft) {
326â†’                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.local_draft))
327â†’            } else if (post.isLocallyChanged) {
328â†’                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.local_changes))
329â†’            }
330â†’            if (postStatus == PRIVATE) {
331â†’                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_status_post_private))
332â†’            }
333â†’            if (postStatus == PENDING) {
334â†’                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_status_pending_review))
335â†’            }
336â†’            if (post.sticky) {
337â†’                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_status_sticky))
338â†’            }
339â†’        }
340â†’        return labels
341â†’    }
342â†’
343â†’    private fun getErrorLabel(uploadUiState: UploadFailed, postStatus: PostStatus): UiString {
344â†’        return when {
345â†’            uploadUiState.error.mediaError != null -> getMediaUploadErrorMessage(uploadUiState, postStatus)
346â†’            uploadUiState.error.postError != null -> UploadUtils.getErrorMessageResIdFromPostError(
347â†’                postStatus,
348â†’                false,
349â†’                uploadUiState.error.postError,
350â†’                uploadUiState.isEligibleForAutoUpload
351â†’            )
352â†’
353â†’            else -> {
354â†’                val errorMsg = "MediaError and postError are both null."
355â†’                if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
356â†’                    throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)
357â†’                } else {
358â†’                    AppLog.e(POSTS, errorMsg)
359â†’                }
360â†’                UiStringRes(R.string.error_generic)
361â†’            }
362â†’        }
363â†’    }
364â†’
365â†’    private fun getMediaUploadErrorMessage(uploadUiState: UploadFailed, postStatus: PostStatus): UiStringRes {
366â†’        return when {
367â†’            uploadUiState.isEligibleForAutoUpload -> when (postStatus) {
368â†’                PUBLISHED -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_published_retrying)
369â†’                PRIVATE -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_published_retrying_private)
370â†’                SCHEDULED -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_scheduled_retrying)
371â†’                PENDING -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_submitted_retrying)
372â†’                DRAFT, TRASHED, UNKNOWN -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_generic_error_retrying)
373â†’            }
374â†’
375â†’            uploadUiState.retryWillPushChanges -> when (postStatus) {
376â†’                PUBLISHED -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_published)
377â†’                PRIVATE -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_published_private)
378â†’                SCHEDULED -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_scheduled)
379â†’                PENDING -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_submitted)
380â†’                DRAFT, TRASHED, UNKNOWN -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post)
381â†’            }
382â†’
383â†’            else -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post)
384â†’        }
385â†’    }

387â†’    private fun shouldShowOverlay(uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState, performingCriticalAction: Boolean): Boolean {
388â†’        // show overlay when post upload is in progress or (media upload is in progress and the user is not using Aztec)
389â†’        return performingCriticalAction ||
390â†’                (uploadUiState is UploadingPost ||
